footer.subtitle: Catholic Church · Youngstown, OH
footer.description: Served by the Dominican Friars of the Province of St. Joseph. Bringing the riches of Christ to Youngstown since 1923.

welcome.title: Encounter Jesus Christ with Us
welcome.p1: St. Dominic Church has served Youngstown's south side since 1923, in the care of the Dominican Friars.
welcome.p2: Whether you're a lifelong Catholic or just curious, you're welcome here.

massCta.title: Find Jesus Here
massCta.desc: Join us! We'd love to welcome you to our beautiful Romanesque church. Whether you're visiting Youngstown or looking for a new church home, find our Mass and Confession times below.

priests.title: Priests & Staff
priests.quote: "To praise, to bless, and to preach."
priests.desc: Our Dominican friars and parish staff are here to help — reach out anytime.

involved.title: Become a Member
involved.desc: There are many ways to get involved — ministries, events, and service. Come be part of the parish.

quotes.label: Daily Reflection
quotes.q1: The truth does not change according to our ability to stomach it.
quotes.q2: A man who governs his passions is master of the world. We must either command them, or be enslaved by them.
quotes.q3: The soul that walks in love neither tires others nor grows tired.
quotes.q4: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.
quotes.q5: Do not be afraid. Do not be satisfied with mediocrity. Put out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch.
quotes.q6: We are not the sum of our weaknesses and failures; we are the sum of the Father's love for us.
quotes.q7: Prayer is the place of refuge for every worry, a foundation for cheerfulness, a source of constant happiness, a protection against sadness.
quotes.q8: You have made us for Yourself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in You.
quotes.q9: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you; all things pass; God does not change. Patience attains all. Whoever has God lacks nothing: God alone suffices.
quotes.q10: My vocation is love. In the heart of the Church, I will be love.
quotes.q11: Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire.
quotes.q12: Have charity one for another, guard humility, and embrace voluntary poverty.
quotes.q13: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Anxiety accomplishes nothing. God is merciful and will hear your prayer.
quotes.q14: Not all of us can do great things. But we can do small things with great love.
quotes.q15: Lord, make me an instrument of Your peace. Where there is hatred, let me sow love.
quotes.q16: Pray as if everything depended on God; work as if everything depended on you.
quotes.q18: Do not abandon yourselves to despair. We are an Easter people, and Hallelujah is our song.
quotes.q19: The world offers you comfort, but you were not made for comfort. You were made for greatness.
quotes.q20: Only love creates. Hatred destroys. To love means to give oneself entirely, without reserve.
quotes.q21: The measure of love is to love without measure.
quotes.q22: Patience is the virtue that allows the soul to bear all things in peace, without losing its trust in God.
quotes.q23: When it comes to life, the critical thing is whether you take things for granted or take them with gratitude.
quotes.q24: Do not consider whose knowledge it is, but bring whatever good you hear to memory.
quotes.q25: In all things seek God, in all things love God, in all things rejoice in God.
quotes.q26: The greatest attribute of God is His mercy. Mankind will not have peace until it turns with trust to Divine Mercy.
quotes.q27: Prayer is nothing else but union with God. It is a sweet conversation of the child with his Father.
quotes.q28: Love the poor. To love them, you must serve them. To serve them, you must approach them with humility.
quotes.q29: Actions speak louder than words; let your words teach and your actions speak.
quotes.q30: Verso l'alto. To the heights. Live, do not merely exist.

word.title: Word
word.desc: Rooted in the Dominican intellectual tradition, we preach the Gospel with clarity and depth through homilies, Bible study, and faith formation.

sacrament.title: Sacrament
sacrament.desc: We celebrate the liturgy with reverence and beauty — daily Mass, frequent Confession, Eucharistic adoration, and all seven sacraments.

service.title: Service
service.desc: From St. Vincent de Paul outreach to mission trips, we serve our neighbors and bring Christ's love to those in need across Youngstown.

vatican.title: News from the Vatican
vatican.privacy: This loads a Vatican News widget from vaticannews.va.

formation.title: Keep Growing After Sunday
formation.desc: Follow trusted Catholic teaching, parish updates, and resources for prayer throughout the week.

massTimes.title: Mass, Confession & Adoration

hero.desc: Join us for the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass, the Sacrament of Reconciliation, and Eucharistic Adoration at St. Dominic Church.

mass.title: Mass Schedule
mass.quote: "The Eucharist is the source and summit of the Christian life."
mass.desc: The Holy Mass is celebrated daily at St. Dominic Church. All are welcome to join us in the most sacred act of Catholic worship.

massTimes.reviewed: Schedule reviewed May 4, 2026. Holy Day schedules may vary; check the bulletin or call the parish office.

parallax1.quote: "The Eucharist is the consummation of the whole spiritual life."

confession.title: Confession Schedule
confession.quote: "The whole power of the sacrament of Penance consists in restoring us to God's grace and joining us with Him in an intimate friendship."
confession.desc: The Sacrament of Reconciliation is one of the great gifts of the Catholic faith. Through Confession, we receive God's mercy and are restored to grace. Our priests are available for Confession during the scheduled times below, or by appointment.
confession.howToDesc: If it has been a while since your last Confession, don't worry — our priests are patient and welcoming. Here is a simple guide:
confession.step1: Examine your conscience before entering the confessional.
confession.step2: Begin by saying: "Bless me, Father, for I have sinned. It has been [length of time] since my last Confession."
confession.step3: Confess your sins honestly and completely.
confession.step4: Listen to the priest's counsel and accept the penance he assigns.
confession.step5: Pray the Act of Contrition when the priest asks you to.
confession.step6: After absolution, complete your penance and give thanks to God for His mercy.
confession.actText: O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended you, and I detest all my sins because of your just punishments, but most of all because they offend you, my God, who are all good and deserving of all my love. I firmly resolve, with the help of your grace, to sin no more and to avoid the near occasions of sin. Amen.

examine.title: Examination of Conscience
examine.desc: Before going to Confession, take time to prayerfully examine your conscience. This examination follows the Ten Commandments, drawn from the Knights of Columbus prayer book in our pews.

c1.title: I am the Lord your God; you shall not have strange gods before me.
items: Do I love God above all things?
Do I pray daily?
Have I been involved with the occult — witchcraft, ouija boards, tarot, or fortune telling?
Have I received Holy Communion in a state of mortal sin?

c2.title: You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain.
items: Have I used God's holy name irreverently?
Have I blasphemed God, the Church, Our Lady, or the saints?

c3.title: Remember to keep holy the Lord's Day.
items: Do I keep Sunday as a day of prayer and rest?
Have I deliberately come late to or left early from Mass?

c4.title: Honor your father and your mother.
items: Do I honor and respect my parents?
Do I treat my children with love and respect?
Do I support the members of my family?

c5.title: You shall not kill.
items: Have I deliberately harmed anyone?
Have I had or encouraged an abortion?
Have I attempted suicide?
Have I abused drugs or alcohol?

c6.title: You shall not commit adultery.
items: Am I faithful to my spouse in thought and action?
Have I used artificial contraception?
Have I engaged in sexual activity outside of marriage?
Have I viewed pornography?

c7.title: You shall not steal.
items: Have I stolen or accepted stolen goods?
Have I deliberately destroyed property?
Have I cheated anyone?
Do I share with the poor?

c8.title: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
items: Have I lied?
Have I gossiped?
Have I ruined another's good name?

c9.title: You shall not desire your neighbor's wife.
items: Have I permitted sexual thoughts about anyone besides my spouse?
Do I guard my imagination and senses?

c10.title: You shall not desire your neighbor's goods.
items: Am I envious of the possessions, abilities, or success of others?

loveNeighbor.title: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
items: Do I love my neighbor?
Do I forgive those who hurt me?
Do I harbor hatred?
Do I pray for my enemies?

precepts.title: Precepts of the Church
items: Have I deliberately missed Mass on a Sunday or Holy Day without serious reason?
Do I go to Confession at least once a year?
Do I receive Holy Communion at least once during Eastertime?
Do I abstain from meat on Fridays in Lent and fast on Ash Wednesday and Good Friday?
Do I fast one hour before Holy Communion? Do I contribute to the support of the Church?

examine.source: From the Knights of Columbus Examination of Conscience

parallax2.quote: "God never tires of forgiving us; we are the ones who tire of seeking His mercy."

adoration.title: Adoration & Devotions
adoration.quote: "In the Most Blessed Sacrament of the Eucharist the body and blood, together with the soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ are truly, really, and substantially contained."
adoration.desc: Eucharistic Adoration is a profound encounter with the living Christ. At St. Dominic, we expose the Blessed Sacrament in a beautiful gold monstrance for the faithful to adore, pray, and spend time in the presence of our Lord.
adoration.rosary: Holy Rosary — prayed before daily Masses
adoration.divineMercy: Divine Mercy Chaplet — Fridays at 3:00 PM

holyDays.title: Holy Days of Obligation
holyDays.desc: Catholics are required to attend Mass on Holy Days of Obligation. At St. Dominic, we celebrate these feasts with a Vigil Mass and multiple Masses throughout the day.
holyDays.schedule: Vigil: 5:00 PM · Holy Day: 8:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 5:00 PM, 7:00 PM (Spanish)
days: Ascension of the Lord — 40 days after Easter

visitor.title: Start with Mass, then let us help
visitor.desc: If you are visiting, returning to the Church, or bringing family for the first time, come as you are. The parish office can help with directions, accessibility, Confession, or next steps after Mass.

cta.title: Come Worship with Us
cta.desc: Whether you're joining us for Mass, spending time in prayer before the Blessed Sacrament, or seeking the healing grace of Confession — you are always welcome at St. Dominic.

history.title: A Legacy of Faith Since 1923
history.p1: The Dominican Order of Preachers founded St. Dominic Church on February 23, 1923, at the invitation of Cleveland Archbishop Joseph Schrembs. The first Mass was offered in a storeroom on Market Street — a humble beginning for what would become a vibrant center of faith on Youngstown's south side.
history.p2: The current building, a stunning limestone-and-brick Romanesque structure, was dedicated on April 28, 1957. A life-size statue of Saint Dominic stands positioned over the entrance, welcoming all who come to worship.
history.p3: Saint Dominic de Guzman founded the Dominican Order in the 13th century, devoted to rekindling the faith of Europe. In the centuries since, Dominicans have spread around the world with their motto: "to praise, to bless, and to preach." Our church carries forward this tradition in the Diocese of Youngstown.
history.p4: Today, St. Dominic maintains many community-focused ministries, including a Lay Dominican program, Catholic Men's Fellowship, Church and Family Life ministry, and a vibrant Hispanic Ministry program with weekly Spanish Masses.
history.motto: "To praise, to bless, and to preach."

word.title: Word
word.desc: We preach the Gospel with clarity and depth, rooted in the Dominican intellectual tradition and the teachings of the Church.

sacrament.title: Sacrament
sacrament.desc: We celebrate the liturgy with reverence and beauty, offering daily Mass, frequent Confession, and Eucharistic adoration.

service.title: Service
service.desc: We serve the poor and our neighbors through outreach, community events, and ministries that bring Christ's love to Youngstown.

architecture.title: Our Church Architecture
architecture.desc: The architectural style of St. Dominic Church is modernized Romanesque — featuring limestone and brick construction, a towering presence on Lucius Avenue, and sacred art that inspires prayer and contemplation.

bios.frassati-davis: Fr. Frassati Davis was born into a military family and calls Bellevue, Nebraska home. He grew up with two older sisters and a younger brother. He attended St. Mary's University of Minnesota, where he was taught by a Dominican Friar of the Central Province and earned a BA in Philosophy. "The natural rhythm of prayer, study, and preaching attracted me to the Order of Preachers, and the joy of the friars living out a consecrated religious vocation drew me deeper into the call to be a son of St. Dominic."
bios.charles-rooney: Fr. Charles Marie Rooney, O.P., was born in New York, NY, and raised in Darien, CT, the eldest of three boys. He graduated with a B.A. from Duke University in 2016 and an M.A. from Maynooth University, Ireland, in 2017 before entering the Dominican Friars of the Province of St. Joseph. He was ordained a priest in 2024 and, before coming to St. Dominic's in July 2025, served in hospital chaplaincy in New York City and completed his Licentiate in Sacred Theology in Washington, DC. He enjoys athletics, music, and languages, and his favorite saint is Pope St. John Paul II.
bios.john-kozlowski: Fr. John Chrysostom Kozlowski, O.P. is a Dominican Friar in residence at St. Dominic Church, contributing to the spiritual life of the community through preaching and sacramental ministry.
bios.bernard-confer: Fr. Bernard Confer, O.P. is a Dominican Friar in residence at St. Dominic Church, serving the community through prayer, preaching, and the celebration of the sacraments.
bios.gregory-salomone: Fr. Gregory Salomone, O.P. is a Dominican Friar in residence at St. Dominic Church, enriching the community through his ministry of preaching and teaching in the Dominican tradition.
bios.sylvia-gould: Sylvia Gould serves as Church Secretary, managing the daily operations of the church office and serving as the first point of contact for members and visitors seeking information or assistance.
bios.alec-ring: Alec Ring serves as Music Director, leading the church music program and enhancing the liturgical worship of the community through sacred music and choral direction.

charles-rooney.text: It is Jesus in fact whom you seek when you dream of happiness; he is waiting for you when nothing else satisfies you; he is the beauty to which you are so attracted; it is he who provokes you with that thirst for fullness that will not let you settle for compromise…

bulletin.title: Weekly Bulletin

hero.desc: Stay connected with the latest announcements, Mass intentions, ministry updates, and the pastor's message.

bulletin.heading: This Week's Bulletin
bulletin.reviewed: Bulletin archive reviewed May 4, 2026. Current bulletins are posted when the parish feed is connected.

fallback.title: Bulletin Coming Soon
fallback.desc: Our weekly bulletin will be embedded here for easy reading. In the meantime, visit our church website for the latest bulletin.

info.title: Bulletin Announcements
info.deadline: Submissions due by Monday at noon
info.deadlineDesc: All bulletin announcements must be submitted to the church office by Monday at noon each week for inclusion in the following weekend's bulletin.
info.contactDesc: To submit an announcement or event for the bulletin, contact the church office by phone or email.

becomingCatholic.title: Interested in Becoming Catholic?
becomingCatholic.heading: Begin Your Journey of Faith
becomingCatholic.desc: Whether you've never been baptized, were baptized in another Christian tradition, or are a baptized Catholic who hasn't yet received Confirmation or the Eucharist — we're here to walk with you.
becomingCatholic.quote: "Thus faith comes from what is heard, and what is heard comes through the word of Christ."

intro.title: Begin Your Journey of Faith
intro.desc: Whether you've never been baptized, were baptized in another Christian tradition, or are a baptized Catholic who hasn't yet received Confirmation or the Eucharist — we're here to walk with you.

new.title: New to the Faith?
new.desc: If you're curious about Catholicism and have questions, we'd love to talk. No pressure, no commitment — just an open conversation about Jesus Christ and His Church.

returning.title: Returning to the Church?
returning.desc: Welcome back. Whether it's been months or decades, the doors are open and the Lord is waiting for you. We'll help you reconnect with the Sacraments and the community.

ready.title: Ready to Begin OCIA?
ready.desc: Our church offers OCIA (Order of Christian Initiation of Adults, formerly RCIA) — an 8-to-9-month journey of faith running September through May. Classes meet weekly, culminating at the Easter Vigil. Available in English and in Spanish (Wednesday evenings at 7:00 PM).

becomingCatholic.reachOutDesc: Contact the church office and we'll connect you with one of our priests or staff who can answer your questions and help you take the next step.

contact.title: Reach Out to Us
contact.desc: Contact the church office and we'll connect you with one of our priests or staff who can answer your questions and help you take the next step toward Baptism or full reception into the Church.

faithFormation.title: Faith Formation
faithFormation.quote: "Be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and pleasing and perfect."

intro.title: Deepen Your Faith
intro.desc: The Dominican tradition places the pursuit of truth at the heart of the spiritual life. Whether you are a lifelong Catholic or just beginning to explore, these resources — rooted in Sacred Scripture, the teachings of the Church, and the wisdom of St. Thomas Aquinas — will help you grow in your relationship with Jesus Christ.

why.title: Why Learn More About Your Faith?
why.p1: Our Lord calls us not only to believe, but to understand what we believe. The more deeply we know our faith, the more powerfully we can live it — and share it with others. Study and prayer go hand in hand; one feeds the other.
why.p2: St. Dominic founded the Order of Preachers so that the fruits of contemplation could be shared with the world. These resources carry on that mission, making the riches of Catholic theology and spirituality accessible to everyone.

resources.title: Explore & Learn
resources.desc: Curated resources from the Dominican intellectual tradition and the wider Catholic Church — theology, podcasts, catechesis, and spiritual reading.

thomistic.title: Thomistic Institute
thomistic.desc: The Thomistic Institute promotes Catholic truth in the public square through academic lectures, campus outreach, and digital content rooted in the thought of St. Thomas Aquinas.

godsplaining.title: Godsplaining
godsplaining.desc: Dominican friars talk all things God — a lively, accessible podcast bringing the richness of Catholic theology to everyday life and modern questions.

rosary.title: Rosary in a Year
rosary.desc: Pray through the mysteries of the Rosary day by day with Fr. Mark-Mary Ames, CFR. A beautiful guided journey into the prayer life of Our Lady.

dominicana.title: Dominicana Journal
dominicana.desc: A journal of theology and spirituality by Dominican student brothers of the Province of St. Joseph — articles on Scripture, philosophy, the saints, and the Dominican life.

saintsAlive.title: Saints Alive!
saintsAlive.desc: Catholic content about the lives of the Saints, perfect for families and children. Discover the heroes of our faith in engaging, story-driven episodes.

catechism.title: Catechism of the Catholic Church
catechism.desc: The definitive summary of Catholic doctrine from the USCCB. An essential reference for understanding what the Church believes, teaches, and proclaims.

cta.title: Start Your Journey Today
cta.desc: Pick a resource, press play on a podcast, or open the Catechism. Every step you take toward understanding your faith is a step closer to Christ.

hero.desc: Homilies, reflections, and parish life from the Dominican community at St. Dominic Church.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.heading: There's a Place for You
getInvolved.desc: St. Dominic Church is a vibrant community with many ways to get involved. Whether you're looking to deepen your prayer life, serve those in need, or build friendships — there's a ministry for you.

liturgical.title: Liturgical Ministries
liturgical.desc: Serve as a lector, extraordinary minister, altar server, sacristan, or choir member.
liturgical.detail: Our liturgical ministries are at the heart of church worship. We welcome volunteers to serve as lectors, extraordinary ministers of Holy Communion, altar servers, sacristans, and members of the liturgical environment committee. Training and formation are provided for all new ministers. Whether you have experience or are just beginning, there is a role for you in making our liturgies beautiful and reverent.

hispanic.title: Hispanic Ministry
hispanic.desc: Join our vibrant Spanish-speaking community with weekly Masses and cultural events.
hispanic.detail: Our Hispanic Ministry serves the growing Spanish-speaking community of St. Dominic Church. We celebrate a weekly Sunday Mass in Spanish at 12:30 PM and host cultural events, prayer groups, and community gatherings throughout the year. The ministry fosters a welcoming environment where Hispanic families can worship, grow in faith, and build lasting friendships within the church.

layDominicans.title: Lay Dominicans
layDominicans.desc: Explore the Dominican charism more deeply as a member of the Lay Dominican Chapter.
layDominicans.detail: The Lay Dominican Chapter at St. Dominic Church invites men and women to live the Dominican charism in their daily lives. Members commit to a rule of life centered on prayer, study, community, and preaching. Through regular meetings, shared prayer, and ongoing formation in Dominican spirituality, lay Dominicans deepen their relationship with God and bring the fruits of contemplation to the world.

familyLife.title: Church & Family Life
familyLife.desc: Help organize community-building events, dinners, and church celebrations.
familyLife.detail: Church & Family Life coordinates community-building events that bring members together outside of Sunday Mass. From church dinners and picnics to holiday celebrations and social gatherings, this ministry helps create the bonds of friendship that make our church a true family. Volunteers are always welcome to help plan and organize these events.

mensFellowship.title: Catholic Men's Fellowship
mensFellowship.desc: Join men of the church for prayer, study, and fellowship.
mensFellowship.detail: The Catholic Men's Fellowship gathers men of the church for prayer, study, and brotherhood. Through regular meetings featuring guest speakers, Scripture study, and shared meals, the fellowship encourages men to grow as Catholic husbands, fathers, and leaders in their families and community.

svdp.title: St. Vincent de Paul Society
svdp.desc: Serve those in need in our community through direct outreach and assistance.
svdp.detail: The St. Vincent de Paul Society is dedicated to serving those in need in our community. Members make home visits, provide emergency assistance with food, utilities, and rent, and connect individuals with additional resources. Following the example of St. Vincent de Paul, we see the face of Christ in every person we serve.

music.title: Music Ministry
music.desc: Share your musical gifts at Mass — choir, cantoring, and instrumentalists welcome.
music.detail: Our Music Ministry enhances the liturgical worship of the church through sacred music. We welcome singers and instrumentalists of all skill levels to join our choir, serve as cantors, or accompany the congregation. Rehearsals are held weekly, and no audition is required — just a love for music and a desire to praise God through song.

religiousEd.title: Religious Education (CCD)
religiousEd.desc: CCD classes serve grades K–8, meeting Sundays 9:00–10:15 AM from September through May.
religiousEd.detail: Our Religious Education program (CCD) provides faith formation for children in grades K through 8. Classes meet on Sundays from 9:00 to 10:15 AM, September through May. Our dedicated catechists guide students through the foundations of the Catholic faith, preparing them for the sacraments and a lifelong relationship with Christ. We are always looking for volunteer catechists and aides.

youth.title: Youth Ministry
youth.desc: High School and Junior High youth groups, plus a Young Families group.
youth.detail: Youth Ministry at St. Dominic serves teens and young families through engaging programs that build faith and community. Our High School and Junior High groups meet regularly for fellowship, service projects, and faith formation. We also offer annual mission trips — a medical mission trip in April and a teen mission trip in June — giving young people the opportunity to serve those in need and grow in their faith.

bibleStudy.title: Bible Study
bibleStudy.desc: Weekly Bible study open to all members in the Dominican tradition.
bibleStudy.detail: Our weekly Bible Study is open to all members who want to deepen their knowledge of Sacred Scripture. In the rich Dominican tradition of study and contemplation, participants explore the biblical texts together, guided by commentary from the Church Fathers and the Magisterium. No prior Bible knowledge is necessary — all are welcome, from beginners to seasoned students of Scripture.

caring.title: Caring Ministries
caring.desc: Reaching out to the sick, homebound, and grieving members of our church family.
caring.detail: Caring Ministries reach out to the sick, homebound, and grieving members of our church family. Volunteers make regular visits, bring Holy Communion, send cards, prepare meals, and offer prayer and companionship during times of difficulty. If you or someone you know is in need of support, or if you would like to volunteer, please contact the church office.

missions.title: Mission Trips
missions.desc: Annual medical mission trip (April) and teen mission trip (June).
missions.detail: St. Dominic Church offers annual mission trips that give members the opportunity to serve those in need beyond our community. Our medical mission trip takes place each April, bringing healthcare professionals and volunteers to underserved areas. In June, our teen mission trip gives young people a transformative experience of service and faith. Both trips are life-changing experiences of living out the Gospel.

explore.title: Explore
explore.desc: Browse our ministries above and find one that matches your interests and gifts.

connect.title: Connect
connect.desc: Call the church office or talk to a priest after Mass to learn more about the ministry.

serve.title: Serve
serve.desc: Show up, get involved, and become part of our church family. There's a place for everyone.

register.title: Ready to Register?
register.desc: Contact the church office to register as a new member. We can't wait to welcome you to our family!

modal.joinCta: Interested? Contact the church office to get started.

contact.title: Contact Us

give.title: Online Giving
give.heading: Your Generosity Makes a Difference
give.desc: Your financial support helps St. Dominic Church continue its mission of preaching, teaching, and serving. Every gift — large or small — helps sustain our liturgies, ministries, and outreach programs.
give.quote: "Each must do as already determined, without sadness or compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver."
give.onlineDesc: Flocknote is a safe, convenient way to make one-time or recurring donations to St. Dominic Church. You can give anytime from your computer or mobile device.
give.comingSoon: Online giving is being configured. In the meantime, please use one of the methods below or contact the church office.
give.contactOffice: Call us to arrange your gift today.
give.flocknoteNote: Payment details are handled by Flocknote's secure checkout. St. Dominic Church does not store card or bank information on this website.
give.clarityDesc: Most parishioners use Flocknote for recurring offertory, but one-time gifts, memorial gifts, and mailed checks remain welcome.
give.feeNote: Processing fees vary by payment method. Choose ACH/bank transfer when available if you want more of your gift to reach the parish.

office.title: Call the Office
office.desc: Ask about envelopes, annual statements, memorial gifts, or stock transfers.

mass.title: Sunday Offertory
mass.desc: Prefer in person? Bring your gift to any weekend Mass.

register.title: Register
register.desc: New parishioners can register so giving records stay connected.

oneTime.title: One-Time Gifts
oneTime.desc: Give once to support parish needs.

recurring.title: Recurring Giving
recurring.desc: Set a weekly or monthly gift.

payments.title: Card or ACH
payments.desc: Use debit, credit, or bank transfer.

offertory.title: Sunday Offertory
offertory.desc: Place your gift in the collection basket during any weekend Mass. Envelopes are available at the church entrance.

mail.title: Give by Mail
mail.desc: Mail your gift to: St. Dominic Church, 77 East Lucius Avenue, Youngstown, OH 44507.

stock.title: Stock & Securities
stock.desc: Transfer appreciated stock or securities for a tax-advantaged gift. Contact the church office for details.

give.taxNote: St. Dominic Catholic Church is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization. All donations are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. You will receive an annual giving statement for your records.

sacraments.title: The Sacraments
sacraments.heading: Encounter Christ Through the Sacraments
sacraments.desc: The seven sacraments are at the heart of Catholic life. They are efficacious signs of grace, instituted by Christ and entrusted to the Church, by which divine life is dispensed to us. At St. Dominic Church, we joyfully celebrate each sacrament.
sacraments.quote: "The sacraments are efficacious signs of grace, instituted by Christ and entrusted to the Church, by which divine life is dispensed to us."

baptism.title: Baptism
baptism.quote: "Go, therefore, and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the holy Spirit."
baptism.brief: The gateway to life in the Spirit. Through Baptism we are freed from sin and reborn as children of God.
baptism.heading: New Life in Christ
baptism.p1: Baptism is the first and fundamental sacrament of Christian initiation. Through the waters of Baptism, we are cleansed of original sin, reborn as children of God, and welcomed into the Catholic Church. It is the doorway through which we enter into life with Christ.
baptism.p2: At St. Dominic Church, Baptism is celebrated at 12:30 PM on the 2nd and 4th Sundays of each month. Baptism preparation classes are held on the 2nd Wednesday of odd-numbered months at 7:00 PM in the church. Contact the church office to register.

req1.title: Baptism Preparation
req1.desc: Parents and godparents attend a preparation session covering the meaning and responsibilities of Baptism.

req2.title: Godparents
req2.desc: At least one godparent must be a confirmed, practicing Catholic in good standing. A baptized non-Catholic Christian may serve as a Christian witness alongside a Catholic godparent.

req3.title: Registration
req3.desc: Contact the church office at least one month before the desired Baptism date to register and schedule your preparation.

baptism.ctaDesc: Contact us to begin the process. We're happy to answer any questions you may have.

firstCommunion.title: First Holy Communion
firstCommunion.quote: "I am the living bread that came down from heaven; whoever eats this bread will live forever."
firstCommunion.brief: Children receive the Body and Blood of Christ for the first time, deepening their union with Jesus.
firstCommunion.heading: Receiving the Body of Christ
firstCommunion.p1: First Holy Communion is a profound moment in a young Catholic's faith journey. It is the first time a child receives the Eucharist — the true Body and Blood of Jesus Christ — and enters into deeper communion with the Lord and the Church.
firstCommunion.p2: At St. Dominic Church, children typically receive First Communion in second grade, after a two-year preparation through our religious education program. The celebration is a joyful occasion for the child, their family, and the entire church community.

prep1.title: Religious Education
prep1.desc: Children participate in our church CCD program beginning in first grade, learning the fundamentals of the Catholic faith.

prep2.title: First Reconciliation
prep2.desc: Before receiving First Communion, children celebrate the Sacrament of Reconciliation (Confession) for the first time.

prep3.title: Family Preparation
prep3.desc: Parents are partners in their child's faith formation. Families attend a preparation session together before the celebration.

firstCommunion.ctaDesc: Contact the church office or our Coordinator of Religious Education to enroll your child in the program.

confirmation.title: Confirmation
confirmation.quote: "You will receive power when the holy Spirit comes upon you, and you will be my witnesses."
confirmation.brief: The fullness of the Holy Spirit strengthens the baptized to spread and defend the faith.
confirmation.heading: Sealed with the Holy Spirit
confirmation.p1: Confirmation completes the grace of Baptism and endows the recipient with the special strength of the Holy Spirit. Through Confirmation, Catholics are more perfectly bound to the Church and enriched with the power to witness to Christ in word and deed.
confirmation.p2: At St. Dominic Church, Confirmation is usually conferred upon students in grade 8, prepared through our CCD program. Adults who have not been confirmed are welcome to contact the church to arrange preparation at any time.

prep1.title: Formation Program
prep1.desc: Candidates participate in a multi-month preparation program of catechesis, prayer, and service to deepen their understanding of the faith.

prep2.title: Sponsor
prep2.desc: Each candidate chooses a sponsor — a confirmed, practicing Catholic who serves as a spiritual mentor and guide.

prep3.title: Service & Witness
prep3.desc: Candidates complete service hours and reflect on how they are called to live out their faith in the world.

confirmation.ctaDesc: Contact us for information about our Confirmation preparation program for teens and adults.

marriage.title: Marriage
marriage.quote: "That is why a man leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife, and the two of them become one body."
marriage.brief: A man and a woman establish a lifelong partnership ordered to the good of the spouses and the procreation of children.
marriage.heading: A Covenant of Love
marriage.p1: The Sacrament of Marriage is a sacred covenant between a man and a woman, witnessed before God and the Church. Through this sacrament, the couple receives the grace to love each other with the selfless, faithful love that mirrors Christ's love for the Church.
marriage.p2: At St. Dominic Church, we are honored to walk with couples as they prepare for the Sacrament of Marriage. Couples should contact the church at least six months before their desired wedding date to begin the preparation process.
marriage.stepsTitle: Steps to Getting Married at St. Dominic

step1.title: Initial Meeting
step1.desc: Meet with one of our priests to discuss your plans, wedding date availability, and the preparation process. Contact us at least six months in advance.

step2.title: Pre-Cana Marriage Preparation
step2.desc: Complete Pre-Cana — three consecutive Sunday evening sessions (offered in winter, spring, or fall) covering communication, decision-making, faith, and family life. Open to members and alumni.

step3.title: Wedding Liturgy
step3.desc: Work with the celebrant to plan your wedding liturgy, including readings, music, and vows. A rehearsal is held before the ceremony.

marriage.ctaDesc: Contact the church office to schedule your initial meeting with one of our priests.

anointing.title: Anointing of the Sick
anointing.quote: "Is anyone among you sick? He should summon the presbyters of the church, and they should pray over him and anoint him with oil in the name of the Lord."
anointing.brief: Comfort and strength from God for those who are seriously ill, facing surgery, or nearing the end of life.
anointing.heading: Healing and Comfort
anointing.p1: The Anointing of the Sick gives the grace of the Holy Spirit to those who are seriously ill or suffering. Through this sacrament, the sick person is united more closely to Christ's redemptive Passion, receives strength and peace, and — if it is God's will — bodily healing.
anointing.p2: This sacrament may be received more than once, and it is not only for those at the point of death. Anyone facing serious illness, surgery, or the challenges of old age is encouraged to receive this sacrament.
anointing.when1: Before a serious surgery or medical procedure
anointing.when2: During a serious illness or hospitalization
anointing.when3: When dealing with the frailty of old age
anointing.when4: In danger of death from illness or injury
anointing.emergencyDesc: If someone is in danger of death and needs a priest, please call the church at any hour.
anointing.routineDesc: For surgery, serious illness, homebound care, or a hospital visit that is not an immediate danger of death, contact the office and we will help arrange a priest.
anointing.ctaDesc: To arrange Anointing of the Sick for yourself or a loved one, contact the church office.

mass.title: Mass & Confession
mass.desc: See daily Mass, Confession, and Adoration times.

funerals.title: Catholic Funerals
funerals.desc: When death has occurred, start here for funeral help.

contact.title: Contact
contact.desc: Reach the parish office for homebound or hospital visits.

funerals.title: Catholic Funerals
funerals.quote: "I am the resurrection and the life; whoever believes in me, even if he dies, will live."
funerals.brief: The Church accompanies the deceased and their loved ones with prayer, commending the soul to God's mercy.
funerals.heading: Hope in the Resurrection
funerals.p1: The Catholic funeral rites are a celebration of the Christian's journey from this life to eternal life. The Church accompanies the deceased and their loved ones with prayer, offering the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass for the repose of the soul and commending the departed to God's loving mercy.
funerals.p2: At St. Dominic Church, our priests and staff walk with families during their time of loss, helping to arrange a funeral liturgy that honors their loved one and offers the comfort of faith. We are here to help in any way we can.

rite1.title: Vigil & Visitation
rite1.desc: A time of prayer and remembrance, typically held the evening before the funeral Mass at the funeral home or church.

rite2.title: Funeral Mass
rite2.desc: The central celebration, where the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass is offered for the repose of the soul of the deceased.

rite3.title: Rite of Committal
rite3.desc: The final prayers at the cemetery, commending the body of the deceased to the earth and the soul to God.

funerals.ctaDesc: Contact the church office as soon as possible after the death of a loved one. We will guide you through every step.
funerals.urgentTitle: Call the parish before finalizing plans
funerals.urgentDesc: A Catholic funeral is coordinated with the family, funeral home, and parish. Please call us early so a priest or staff member can help with dates, liturgy, music, and next steps.

anointing.title: Anointing of the Sick
anointing.desc: If someone is gravely ill or near death, call for a priest.

mass.title: Mass & Confession
mass.desc: Find regular parish prayer and sacramental times.

contact.title: Parish Office
contact.desc: Ask about funeral planning, records, and pastoral care.

visit.quote: "Do not neglect hospitality, for through it some have unknowingly entertained angels."

hero.title: Plan Your Visit
hero.desc: Whether you're new to the area, exploring the Catholic faith, or just curious — you are welcome here. Here's everything you need to know for your first time at St. Dominic.

steps.title: Sunday at St. Dominic
steps.intro: Come as you are — here's what a Sunday morning with us is like.

dress.title: What Should I Wear?
dress.desc: There's no dress code. Most people dress casually or in Sunday best. Come as you are — what matters is that you're here.

children.title: Can I Bring My Kids?
children.desc: Absolutely! Children are always welcome at Mass. Don't worry if they get a little noisy — we're a family church and we love having young ones here.

language.title: Spanish Mass Available
language.desc: We celebrate a Spanish-language Mass every Sunday at 12:30 PM. Our Hispanic Ministry community is vibrant and welcoming — ¡todos son bienvenidos!

parking.title: Parking & Accessibility
parking.desc: Free parking is available in the lot adjacent to the church. The church is accessible with a ramp entrance on the side of the building.

schedule.title: Join Us This Weekend
schedule.desc: We celebrate Mass every day and four times across the weekend. Our Dominican friars are known for their preaching — come hear the Word proclaimed.
schedule.confessionNote: Confessions: Wed & Fri 11:15 AM–12:00 PM · Sat 11:15 AM–12:00 PM & 4:00–5:00 PM

directions.title: Getting to St. Dominic
directions.parking: Free parking is available in the lot adjacent to the church on Lucius Avenue. The church is accessible with a ramp entrance on the side of the building.

cta.title: We Can't Wait to Meet You
cta.desc: Whether you've been Catholic your whole life or have never stepped foot in a church, our doors are open. The Dominican Friars and the people of St. Dominic Church are here to welcome you home.

placeholder.desc: We're gathering beautiful images of our church, our community, and our worship. Check back soon!

youtube.title: Dominican Preaching
youtube.desc: Explore the teachings and reflections of Fray Nelson Medina, O.P. — a Dominican friar whose video ministry reaches hundreds of thousands of faithful across the Spanish-speaking world.
youtube.privacy: This loads an embedded YouTube player.

history.title: A Century of Faith
history.heroDesc: From a humble storeroom on Market Street to a Romanesque landmark on Youngstown's South Side — the story of St. Dominic Church.

founding.label: The Founding
founding.title: A Humble Beginning on Market Street
founding.p1: The Dominican Order of Preachers founded St. Dominic Church on February 23, 1923, at the invitation of Cleveland Archbishop Joseph Schrembs. The first Mass was celebrated in a modest storeroom at the corner of Market Street and Ravenwood Avenue — a humble beginning for what would become one of Youngstown's most enduring centers of faith.
founding.p2: By 1924, the growing community had outgrown the storefront. A combination church, school, and auditorium was erected on Auburndale Avenue, just off Southern Boulevard, to serve the families of the South Side. The school was staffed by Dominican Sisters from Akron, and by 1929 a new wing and auditorium were added to accommodate the surge of enrollment.
founding.p3: In 1939, a three-story convent was built to house the teaching sisters, and by the early 1950s the school had reached a peak enrollment of approximately 1,300 students. The church was thriving — a reflection of Youngstown's booming steel industry and the waves of immigrant families who had settled on the South Side.
founding.storeAlt: The storefront where the first Mass was said in 1923

building.label: Construction
building.title: Building a House of God
building.p1: Under the leadership of Fr. Joseph O'Connell, O.P., who served as pastor from 1942 to 1956, the church embarked on an ambitious building program. A new priory for the Dominican friars was completed in 1952, and construction of the current church began shortly after — a monumental project that would take several years to complete.
building.p2: The Romanesque structure rose from the earth in limestone and brick, its soaring arches taking shape through the labor of the churchioners and local craftsmen. The design features over eighteen semi-circular Romanesque arches, a hallmark of the medieval style that the Dominican Order has favored for centuries.
building.p3: On Christmas 1955, even before the building was fully complete, the first Midnight Mass was celebrated in the new church — a moment of profound joy for a community that had waited decades for a permanent spiritual home.
building.foundationAlt: Workers laying the church foundation in the early 1950s
building.constructAlt: The church interior with arches under construction
building.exteriorAlt: Church exterior under construction with steel roof frame
building.exteriorCap: The church exterior with steel roof trusses in place

dedication.label: Dedication
dedication.title: A Landmark Dedicated
dedication.p1: The current church was officially dedicated on April 28, 1957. A life-size statue of Saint Dominic was positioned over the main entrance, welcoming all who come to worship. The following year, a new school building was opened with 14 classrooms, a library, gymnasium, and cafeteria — six additional classrooms were later added to meet demand.
dedication.p2: The interior of the church is a masterwork of sacred art. Above the arches, twelve symbols derived from the titles given to the Blessed Virgin Mary in the Litany of Loreto are interspersed with black and white Dominican crosses. The sanctuary features a magnificent mural of the Madonna and Child surrounded by Dominican saints — a focal point that has inspired generations of worshippers.
dedication.completedAlt: The completed church entrance decorated for Christmas
dedication.completedCap: The completed church with its carved tympanum and rose window
dedication.altarboysAlt: Altar boys posed before the Dominican mural, circa 1963
dedication.altarboysCap: Altar boys before the sanctuary mural — c. 1963

growth.label: Change & Renewal
growth.title: Through Challenge and Transformation
growth.p1: The 1960s and 1970s brought sweeping changes to both the Church and the city. Following the Second Vatican Council, the church renovated its worship space in 1978-1979 to reflect the new liturgical directives. The Dominican Sisters, who had faithfully staffed the school since the 1930s, departed in 1979.
growth.p2: The city of Youngstown was also transforming. On September 19, 1977 — a day known as "Black Monday" — the Youngstown Sheet and Tube Company abruptly closed its Campbell Works, laying off over 5,000 workers. Within a few years, every major steel plant in the valley had shut down. A mass exodus of families followed, and the South Side neighborhood around St. Dominic experienced severe blight, rising crime, and population loss.
growth.p3: Yet the church endured. In 2009, Fr. Gregory Maturi, O.P. launched "Operation Redemption," a comprehensive neighborhood renewal program that became a model for faith-based community development. Working with federal, state, and local partners, the church demolished approximately 80 abandoned houses in a four-block radius, converted vacant lots to green spaces, and hosted monthly family events drawing hundreds of families. The school building, closed in 1999, was sold and reopened as a charter school serving approximately 400 children.
growth.massCap: Concelebrated Mass following the Vatican II renovations — 1979

today.label: A Living Church
today.title: Into Our Second Century
today.p1: In May 2023, St. Dominic celebrated its centennial with a Mass celebrated by Bishop David Bonnar and a banquet attended by over 230 members. One hundred years after that first Mass in a storeroom, the church remains a beacon of faith on Youngstown's South Side — now serving over 700 registered households from across the Mahoning Valley.
today.p2: The church maintains a vibrant array of ministries rooted in the Dominican charism: a Lay Dominican program, Catholic Men's Fellowship, Church and Family Life ministry, St. Vincent de Paul Society, and a thriving Hispanic Ministry with weekly Spanish Masses. Dominican friars from the Priory also serve St. Rose Church in Girard and the Youngstown State University Newman Center.
today.p3: Served by the friars of the Province of St. Joseph — the oldest Dominican province in the Americas, founded in 1805 — St. Dominic Church carries forward an 800-year tradition of preaching, prayer, and service in the heart of the Mahoning Valley.
today.aerialAlt: Aerial view of the St. Dominic church campus today
today.aerialCap: The St. Dominic campus today — over 100 years of faith on Youngstown's South Side

register.desc: Welcome to St. Dominic Church! Please complete the form below to register as a member. We look forward to welcoming you into our community.

notFound.title: Page Not Found

arch.title: Architecture & Sacred Art

hero.desc: Explore the Romanesque beauty, sacred art, and Dominican symbolism of St. Dominic Church — a masterpiece of faith built by a community.

exterior.title: The Exterior & Facade
exterior.p1: St. Dominic Church presents a striking example of Modernized Romanesque architecture with Gothic elements, built of warm brick and Indiana limestone. The facade rises to a peaked gable crowned by a Cross of Thorns — a symbol of Christ's Passion — above the great rose window.
exterior.p2: The main entrance features twin arched doorways flanked by carved stone. The papal coat of arms is carved above the left door and the diocesan coat of arms above the right. Between them, in a recessed niche, stands a statue of St. Dominic holding a lily and the Book of the Gospels.
exterior.p3: At street level, the church sign features a marble relief depicting the Blessed Virgin Mary presenting the Rosary to St. Dominic — the foundational scene of Dominican spirituality.
exterior.facadeAlt: St. Dominic Church exterior facade with rose window and limestone entrance
exterior.facadeCap: The limestone facade of St. Dominic Church, completed in 1954
exterior.doorsAlt: Main entrance doors with papal and diocesan coats of arms
exterior.doorsCap: Twin arched doorways with papal and diocesan coats of arms
exterior.signAlt: Church sign with marble relief of the Rosary scene
exterior.signCap: Marble relief: the Blessed Virgin presenting the Rosary to St. Dominic

entrance.title: The Main Entrance
entrance.p1: Approaching the church, visitors are drawn upward by the magnificent rose window — a circular masterpiece of stained glass depicting the four Evangelists with their traditional symbols surrounding Christ at the center. The window's tracery forms a Dominican cross pattern.
entrance.p2: The cornerstone, set into the limestone at the base of the church, reads "St. Dominic's Church 1954," marking the year of completion after two years of construction. The church itself was founded in 1923 by the Dominican Friars.
entrance.photoAlt: Close-up of entrance showing rose window, St. Dominic statue, and carved coats of arms
entrance.photoCap: The entrance in detail: rose window, St. Dominic statue, and carved coats of arms

arch.roseQuote: I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.

nave.title: The Nave
nave.p1: Stepping inside, the eye is immediately drawn upward by the soaring height of the nave. Eighteen Romanesque arches march down both sides of the church, resting on solid brick pillars. Interspersed among the arches are twelve symbols from the Litany of Loreto — ancient titles of the Blessed Virgin Mary — alternating with Dominican crosses.
nave.p2: The carved Stations of the Cross are set between the arches along both walls, inviting the faithful to walk the Way of the Cross. Above, the beamed oak ceiling displays the coats of arms of each Dominican province, along with the three pillars of the Order's mission: Laudare, Benedicere, Praedicare — To Praise, To Bless, To Preach.
nave.p3: The side aisles feature a series of Romanesque arched openings that create a beautiful rhythm of light and shadow, leading to the various shrines and devotional spaces along the perimeter of the church. Votive candles flicker before statues of the saints.
nave.aisleAlt: Side aisle with Romanesque arches, shrines, and votive candles
nave.aisleCap: The side aisle — Romanesque arches frame shrines and votive candles

sanctuary.title: The Sanctuary
sanctuary.p1: The sanctuary is the spiritual heart of the church. The marble high altar stands on a floor of black and white marble — the traditional colors of the Dominican Order. Six bronze candlesticks flank the tabernacle.
sanctuary.p2: Dominating the sanctuary is the great mural depicting the Blessed Virgin Mary and St. Dominic receiving the Rosary, surrounded by seven Dominican saints. This monumental painting fills the entire apse and draws the eye of every visitor who enters the church.
sanctuary.p3: The free-standing marble pulpit, carved by the Italian sculptor Gherardi in 1979, bears the image of St. Thomas Aquinas — the great Dominican theologian and Doctor of the Church. Above the pulpit hangs a large oak crucifix. The sanctuary walls feature Trinity symbolism: the Hand of God, the Pelican (a symbol of Christ's sacrifice), and the Dove of the Holy Spirit on one side, with Faith, Hope, and Charity on the other.
sanctuary.photoAlt: View of the sanctuary from the choir loft showing the great mural, marble altar, and arches
sanctuary.photoCap: The sanctuary from the choir loft — the great Rosary mural, marble altar, and soaring arches

arch.sanctuaryQuote: Laudare, Benedicere, Praedicare — To Praise, To Bless, To Preach.
arch.sanctuaryQuoteSrc: Motto of the Order of Preachers

choirLoft.title: The Choir Loft & Rose Window
choirLoft.p1: The choir loft occupies the rear gallery of the church, offering a commanding view of the nave and sanctuary below. From here, the full grandeur of the architecture reveals itself — the rhythm of the arches, the painted ceiling panels, and the distant glow of the sanctuary mural.
choirLoft.p2: The great rose window, seen from within, transforms sunlight into a kaleidoscope of color. Its stained glass panels depict the four Evangelists — Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John — with their traditional symbols: the angel, the lion, the ox, and the eagle. At the center sits Christ in Majesty.
choirLoft.p3: Flanking the rose window are two murals by the sculptor Gherardi, depicting the great Church Fathers alongside the Blessed Virgin Mary and St. Joseph. Below, the Wurlitzer organ fills the loft, its pipes reaching toward the painted ceiling.
choirLoft.loftAlt: Choir loft interior showing rose window, painted ceiling panels, and organ
choirLoft.loftCap: The choir loft — rose window, painted oak ceiling with Dominican coats of arms, and organ pipes
choirLoft.roseAlt: Rose window interior with stained glass depicting the four Evangelists
choirLoft.roseCap: The rose window from within — the four Evangelists surround Christ in Majesty

campus.title: A Place of Prayer, Study, & Community
campus.desc: The church is connected to the Dominican priory by a cloister walk featuring Eric Gill's Stations of the Cross etchings. Together with the church hall and school buildings, the campus forms a complete center for Dominican life — prayer, study, community, and preaching.
